2606.12073.title: "That's AI Slop, You Bot!" Studying Accusations, Evidence, and Credibility in Online Discourse Towards LLM-Generated Comments
2606.12073.paper_summary: Generative AI has made fluent prose cheap to produce, breaking the old promise to readers that good writing meant real thinking. How have readers responded, and what can this tell us about changing anti-AI attitudes? We analyzed 25 million comments from Hacker News and Reddit (2023-2026), combining LLM judgment on 7,500 sampled accusations of AI use, sentiment trajectories, speech-act coding of 300 confirmed accusations of AI use, and a matched-control test of accused versus non-accused parent comments. We found that the pejorative-label share of accusations rose more than tenfold on both platforms while a placebo vocabulary of pre-2022 inauthenticity terms (shill, astroturf) did not. This shift reflected a fast-growing trend of branding any suspicious or seemingly inauthentic prose as "AI slop". The slop frame now constitutes 94 percent of pejorative mentions, with the dominant comments shifting in tone from mockery toward gatekeeping and structural protest. The key surprise comes from a matched-control test which found that prose features that statistically distinguish AI from human text do not predict which human text gets accused as AI. The new accusations work as social gatekeeping of perceived authenticity without actually screening for AI. This research extends signaling theory by showing that substitute signals used socially can grow even when inaccurate if the underlying detection problem cannot be solved at the non-expert level. It shows that AI's effects on writing from the reader side are distinct from those on the production (writer) side. Detection technology cannot resolve this dynamic because the social function of accusations is increasingly to perform social gatekeeping and in-group signaling as opposed to identifying AI-generated writing.
2606.12073.paper_summary_zh: 生成式人工智慧使流暢的散文變得便宜易產生，打破了對讀者的舊承諾，即好的寫作意味著真實的思考。讀者是如何回應的，這能告訴我們關於變化中的反人工智慧態度什麼？我們分析了來自Hacker News和Reddit的2500萬條評論（2023-2026），結合了對7500個AI使用指控的LLM判斷、情感軌跡、300個確認的AI使用指控的言語行為編碼，以及被指控與未被指控的父母評論的匹配對照測試。我們發現，兩個平台上帶有貶義標籤的指控比例增長了十倍以上，而2022年前的虛假術語（如shill、astroturf）的安慰劑詞彙則沒有。這一變化反映了一種快速增長的趨勢，將任何可疑或似乎不真實的散文品牌化為「AI垃圾」。垃圾框架現在佔據了94%的貶義提及，主導評論的語調從嘲諷轉向了把關和結構性抗議。最大的驚訝來自於一項匹配對照測試，該測試發現統計上區分AI與人類文本的散文特徵並不能預測哪些人類文本會被指控為AI。這些新的指控作為對感知真實性的社會把關，而並非實際篩選AI。這項研究擴展了信號理論，顯示社會上使用的替代信號即使不準確，也能增長，前提是基礎的檢測問題無法在非專家層面上解決。它顯示，人工智慧對寫作的影響從讀者的角度與對生產（作者）側的影響是不同的。檢測技術無法解決這一動態，因為指控的社會功能越來越多地是進行社會把關和內部群體信號，而不是識別AI生成的寫作。
2606.12073.author: Jason Miklian et.al.
